summary: ts-form-utils ships system-info exfiltration in lib/perf.js
details: The package's lib/perf.js combines os.hostname() collection, child_process.execSync, and https.request POSTs to an external host — the canonical system-info / command-exfiltration shape. The file uses curl invocations alongside hostname enumeration (L44, L90, L174) and posts data via https.request (L46, L50). The package is advertised as a TypeScript form-utilities library; outbound network and host enumeration have no relation to the declared purpose. Combined with require('os'), require('http'), require('https'), and require('child_process') at the top of the same file plus execSync at L69, the structure matches an active-attack exfiltration module rather than form-handling code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
